reasoning: As there are no specific resolution dates listed, I will provide a single estimate for the question close date (early Feb 2026) compared to the Nov 2025 value. The TGA balance on Oct 30, 2025 was very high ($905B), which is above the typical Treasury quarterly targets of $750B-$850B. This suggests a likely gradual drawdown or mean reversion. Furthermore, late January and early February are traditionally periods of net outflows due to the beginning of the tax refund season, draining the TGA. There is no structural reason to expect the Treasury to build the cash buffer significantly beyond $900B unless there are specific, unforeseen debt ceiling concerns, which would actually drain the TGA instead. Thus, a decrease is more likely than an increase.
